# UC-18.3.11 · EVPN/VXLAN Tunnel Health

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Availability, Performance &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We blend the control-plane story with the dataplane counters so a wobbly VTEP peer or growing encapsulation errors show up in one place before apps stall or VLANs start flapping.*

---

## Description

Correlates **BGP EVPN** neighbor posture with **NVE**/**VXLAN** operational state and encapsulation fault counters per **VTEP** pair and **VNI**, pulling **Cisco** **NX-OS** `show nve` families, **Arista** **VXLAN** interfaces, **BGP** **l2vpn evpn** sessions, and **NX-OS** **syslog** crumbs into one triage surface so overlay control-plane gossip and dataplane drops cannot contradict each other silently.

## Value

Fabric teams shorten **MTTR** on **silent blackholes** and **half-open overlays**: operations see when **EVPN** says **Established** yet **NVE** peers disagree, when **VNI** segments are **admin-down** while peers look **healthy**, or when **encap**/**decap** counters diverge enough to predict customer-visible **loss** before **helpdesk** volume spikes—freeing **capacity** dollars for real upgrades instead of **war-room** thrash around **telemetry** blind spots.

## Implementation

Land **1546** JSON for **`nxos:nve_peers`**, **`nxos:nve_counters`**, and **`network:vxlan_tunnel`**; keep **`network:bgp_neighbor`** **EVPN** rows co-temporal; add **`cisco:nxos:syslog`**. Normalize **`peer_ip`**, **`vni`**, **`nve_peer_state`**, **`encap_errors`**, **`decap_errors`**. Save **`uc_18_3_11_overlay_health_core`** plus alert clone with **`triage_bucket`** thresholds and **`fabric_site_pod_map`** lookups.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
Place the **Splunk REST API Modular Input** (**Splunkbase app 1546**) on hardened **Heavy Forwarders** that terminate corporate TLS to NX-API, eAPI, or an automation gateway so **show nve peers**, **show nve vni**, **show nve interface**, and Arista **show interfaces vxlan 1** transcripts land as structured JSON beside existing **network:bgp_neighbor** polls inside index **dc_fabric** or **network_ops**. Route **Cisco Data Center Networking App for Splunk** (**7777**) reference dashboards only after you confirm **CMDB** hostnames equal the leaf names printed by NVE CLI; otherwise duplicate the panel SPL with explicit host aliases. Where APIC-adjacent syslog still appears, **Splunk Add-on for Cisco ACI** (1897) may emit **cisco:nxos:syslog** narratives referencing **tunnelIf** or **nvoEp** MO classes; fence those hybrid rows away from standalone VXLAN counters to avoid phantom VNI duplicates. If tenant moves routinely explain false churn, add **Splunk Add-on for VMware** (3215) and **Splunk Add-on for VMware NSX** (4241) guests so you can align **L2VNI** spikes with real **vMotion** calendars. Maintain **fabric_site_pod_map.csv** with leaf, fabric_site, pod_label, region_code, maint_owner plus **nve_peer_allow_down.csv** for labs and decommissioned peers that stay administratively down on purpose. Document NX-OS, EOS, and APIC software trains next to every collector because spelling of nve_peer_state changes subtly across maintenance releases; note any **FIELDALIAS** hotfix in the same ServiceNow entry you used for the change window. Grant **fabric_operator** roles search-only access and keep secrets in **passwords.conf** references instead of Slack snippets.

Step 1 Configure data collection
Schedule **NX-API** **ins_api** calls every 60–300s per leaf based on blast-radius policy, emitting sourcetype **nxos:nve_peers** with peer_ip, **nve_peer_state**, vni, and **ingress replication** peer lists whenever the platform exposes them. Mirror show nve interface statistics into **nxos:nve_counters** with rolling **encap_errors** and **decap_errors** even if you must split VNI detail into a sibling sourcetype; this search expects counters adjacent to peer context. **Arista teams** should issue the same cadence through **eAPI**, mapping show interfaces vxlan 1 into **network:vxlan_tunnel** with oper_state plus tx or rx drops coerced through **EVAL** into **encap_errors** and **decap_errors** semantics for apples-to-apples math. Continue polling network:bgp_neighbor focusing on AFI or SAFI rows that clearly reference **l2vpn evpn**, retaining **bgp_state**, uptime, and the neighbor address that should match peer_ip after normalization. Enable cisco:nxos:syslog on facility **local7** for mnemonic NVE or VXLAN traps that never became JSON; suppress debug classes before fleet rollout. Log **HTTP status**, latency, and gzip ratio per poll into a lightweight sidecar index so support can prove whether silence equals health or a dead poller. For **brownfield** onboarding, backfill a week of **nxos:nve_peers** snapshots via **oneshot** so baselines exist before alerts arm.

Step 2 Create the search
Import the SPL from the spl field of this JSON, wrap the opening index union inside macro %evpn_vxlan_tunnel_idx%, and save **uc_18_3_11_overlay_health_core** as a report with a five-minute refresh for operations wallboards. Clone **uc_18_3_11_overlay_health_alert** by appending filters on **triage_bucket** plus a lookup against **nve_peer_allow_down** so maintenance peers stop paging. Mirror the finalized SPL into Git whenever someone edits the saved search in the UI so drift is visible in pull requests.

**Understanding this SPL**
The five sourcetype OR reflects how data usually arrives: structured NVE peers, counter scrapes, Arista tunnel interfaces, sparse syslog, and BGP neighbor polls. coalesce guards renaming mistakes from Ansible. **evpn_neighbor** ensures Idle IPv4 peers that are unrelated to EVPN never trip the overlay logic. **health_flag** ranks peer or VNI down events ahead of pure counter noise so runbooks open in sensible order. bin _time span=5m absorbs REST jitter without hiding sustained failures. **eventstats perc90** flags leaves that are statistical outliers on encap_errors_total even if every peer claims Up. lookup **fabric_site_pod_map** decorates rows for regional bridges. **triage_bucket** strings should flow into **PagerDuty** custom details so **NOC** routers pick L2 versus L3 escalation paths quickly.

```spl
(index=dc_fabric OR index=network_ops OR index=network)
(
  sourcetype="nxos:nve_peers" OR sourcetype="nxos:nve_counters" OR
  sourcetype="network:vxlan_tunnel" OR sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog" OR
  sourcetype="network:bgp_neighbor"
)
earliest=-4h@m latest=now
| eval leaf=trim(coalesce(host,device_name,switch_name,nms_hostname,dvc,""))
| eval peer_ip=trim(coalesce(peer_ip,peer_vtep_ip,vtep_addr,bgp_neighbor_ip,neighbor,""))
| eval vni_key=tonumber(coalesce(vni,l2vni,vni_id,vxlan_vni,l2_vni,""))
| eval nve_state=lower(trim(coalesce(nve_peer_state,peer_oper_state,vtep_peer_state,"")))
| eval encap_err=tonumber(coalesce(encap_errors,encapsulation_errors,total_encap_drops,inb_encap_err,outb_encap_err,"0"))
| eval decap_err=tonumber(coalesce(decap_errors,decapsulation_errors,total_decap_drops,inb_decap_err,outb_decap_err,"0"))
| eval vxlan_state=lower(trim(coalesce(vni_state,nve_vni_state,tunnel_oper_state,vxlan_line_state,"")))
| eval bgp_fsm=lower(trim(coalesce(bgp_state,neighbor_state,session_state,bgp_fsm_state,"")))
| eval **evpn_neighbor**=if(sourcetype="network:bgp_neighbor" AND match(lower(_raw),"(?i)l2vpn.*evpn|address.family.*evpn"),1,0)
| eval syslog_nve_hit=if(sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog" AND match(_raw,"(?i)nve|vxlan|tunnel|encap|decap"),1,0)
| eval peer_down=if(match(nve_state,"down|idle|inactive|disabled|false|notconn"),1,0)
| eval vni_down=if(match(vxlan_state,"down|inactive|fault|lowerlayerdown|notconnect"),1,0)
| eval bgp_bad=if(sourcetype=="network:bgp_neighbor" AND **evpn_neighbor**==1 AND match(bgp_fsm,"idle|active|connect|opensent|openconfirm"),1,0)
| eval counter_bad=if(encap_err>0 OR decap_err>0,1,0)
| eval **health_flag**=case(peer_down==1 OR vni_down==1 OR bgp_bad==1,3,counter_bad==1,2,syslog_nve_hit==1,1,true(),0)
| where **health_flag**>0 OR counter_bad==1
| bin _time span=5m
| stats max(**health_flag**) AS health_flag_max latest(nve_state) AS nve_peer_state latest(vxlan_state) AS vni_oper_state latest(bgp_fsm) AS bgp_neighbor_state sum(encap_err) AS encap_errors_total sum(decap_err) AS decap_errors_total max(encap_err) AS encap_peak max(decap_err) AS decap_peak values(sourcetype) AS evidence_lanes earliest(_time) AS first_seen latest(_time) AS last_seen BY _time leaf peer_ip vni_key
| where peer_ip!="" AND leaf!=""
| **eventstats perc90**(encap_errors_total) AS fabric_encap_p90
| eval encap_ratio=if(fabric_encap_p90>0,round(encap_errors_total/fabric_encap_p90,2),null())
| eval **triage_bucket**=case(health_flag_max>=3,"control_plane_or_peer_down",health_flag_max==2 AND encap_ratio>=2,"dataplane_counter_surge",health_flag_max==2,"dataplane_counter_noisy",true(),"syslog_or_marginal")
| lookup **fabric_site_pod_map** leaf OUTPUTNEW pod_label fabric_site
| sort - health_flag_max, encap_errors_total, decap_errors_total
| head 500
```

**Step 3 Validate**
On a labeled maintenance leaf, compare show nve peers CLI State columns with latest nve_peer_state in Splunk tolerating one five-minute poll lag. Align **show nve vni** state with **vni_oper_state** for the same VNIs. Arista validation uses **show vxlan vtep** and show interfaces vxlan 1 counters versus **network:vxlan_tunnel** rows. Match network:bgp_neighbor **Established** uptime with show bgp l2vpn evpn summary for the identical peer_ip. When encap_errors_total is non-zero, capture **ASIC** level show commands appropriate to your line card to prove hardware counters move with Splunk. Reject datasets that emit **duplicate** leaf plus peer_ip rows per bin; that almost always means **duplicate** **REST** inputs.

**Step 4 Operationalize**
Build dashboard row one as a **timechart** summing encap_errors_total and decap_errors_total by fabric_site. Row two shows a sortable table of leaf, peer_ip, vni_key, nve_peer_state, vni_oper_state, bgp_neighbor_state, encap_errors_total, decap_errors_total, and triage_bucket ordered by health_flag_max. Row three maps **encap_ratio** distributions or pod level choropleths when **region_code** exists. Add drilldowns that open evidence_lanes raw events. Publish a weekly review checklist that tracks **encap_ratio** histogram shifts. Document **RACI** with **DC switching** owning nve_peer_state, routing owning bgp_neighbor_state, and automation owning collector uptime.

**Step 5 Operationalize & Troubleshoot**
Visualization: render **control_plane_or_peer_down** tiles in deep red, **dataplane_counter_surge** in orange, **dataplane_counter_noisy** in yellow-green, and **syslog_or_marginal** in grey; stack sparklines of encap_errors_total per leaf behind the table. Alert design: schedule every ten minutes across earliest=-70m; fire when health_flag_max stays at three for two consecutive executions or when triage_bucket equals **dataplane_counter_surge** with encap_errors_total above lookup thresholds; throttle forty-five minutes per leaf, peer_ip, and vni_key; honor **maintenance_calendar** suppressions. Alert bodies must carry fabric_site, pod_label, peer_ip, vni_key, state triplets, encap_errors_total, decap_errors_total, and evidence_lanes without API secrets. Troubleshooting: if counters go quiet during an outage, validate Splunk REST Modular Input 1546 queue depth and indexer ingest latency first. Persistent bgp_neighbor_state Established with peer_down flags suggests wrong address-family association or split underlay; deep dive show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbors. vni_down with warm peers often tracks SVI admin events not transport faults. Rising decap_errors_total with flat encap_errors_total may indicate remote **PATH MTU** blackholes or **split horizon** filter learning; pair with WAN path tests before reopening code blue.

## SPL

```spl
(index=dc_fabric OR index=network_ops OR index=network)
(
  sourcetype="nxos:nve_peers" OR sourcetype="nxos:nve_counters" OR
  sourcetype="network:vxlan_tunnel" OR sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog" OR
  sourcetype="network:bgp_neighbor"
)
earliest=-4h@m latest=now
| eval leaf=trim(coalesce(host,device_name,switch_name,nms_hostname,dvc,""))
| eval peer_ip=trim(coalesce(peer_ip,peer_vtep_ip,vtep_addr,bgp_neighbor_ip,neighbor,""))
| eval vni_key=tonumber(coalesce(vni,l2vni,vni_id,vxlan_vni,l2_vni,""))
| eval nve_state=lower(trim(coalesce(nve_peer_state,peer_oper_state,vtep_peer_state,"")))
| eval encap_err=tonumber(coalesce(encap_errors,encapsulation_errors,total_encap_drops,inb_encap_err,outb_encap_err,"0"))
| eval decap_err=tonumber(coalesce(decap_errors,decapsulation_errors,total_decap_drops,inb_decap_err,outb_decap_err,"0"))
| eval vxlan_state=lower(trim(coalesce(vni_state,nve_vni_state,tunnel_oper_state,vxlan_line_state,"")))
| eval bgp_fsm=lower(trim(coalesce(bgp_state,neighbor_state,session_state,bgp_fsm_state,"")))
| eval evpn_neighbor=if(sourcetype="network:bgp_neighbor" AND match(lower(_raw),"(?i)l2vpn.*evpn|address.family.*evpn"),1,0)
| eval syslog_nve_hit=if(sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog" AND match(_raw,"(?i)nve|vxlan|tunnel|encap|decap"),1,0)
| eval peer_down=if(match(nve_state,"down|idle|inactive|disabled|false|notconn"),1,0)
| eval vni_down=if(match(vxlan_state,"down|inactive|fault|lowerlayerdown|notconnect"),1,0)
| eval bgp_bad=if(sourcetype=="network:bgp_neighbor" AND evpn_neighbor==1 AND match(bgp_fsm,"idle|active|connect|opensent|openconfirm"),1,0)
| eval counter_bad=if(encap_err>0 OR decap_err>0,1,0)
| eval health_flag=case(peer_down==1 OR vni_down==1 OR bgp_bad==1,3,counter_bad==1,2,syslog_nve_hit==1,1,true(),0)
| where health_flag>0 OR counter_bad==1
| bin _time span=5m
| stats max(health_flag) AS health_flag_max latest(nve_state) AS nve_peer_state latest(vxlan_state) AS vni_oper_state latest(bgp_fsm) AS bgp_neighbor_state sum(encap_err) AS encap_errors_total sum(decap_err) AS decap_errors_total max(encap_err) AS encap_peak max(decap_err) AS decap_peak values(sourcetype) AS evidence_lanes earliest(_time) AS first_seen latest(_time) AS last_seen BY _time leaf peer_ip vni_key
| where peer_ip!="" AND leaf!=""
| eventstats perc90(encap_errors_total) AS fabric_encap_p90
| eval encap_ratio=if(fabric_encap_p90>0,round(encap_errors_total/fabric_encap_p90,2),null())
| eval triage_bucket=case(health_flag_max>=3,"control_plane_or_peer_down",health_flag_max==2 AND encap_ratio>=2,"dataplane_counter_surge",health_flag_max==2,"dataplane_counter_noisy",true(),"syslog_or_marginal")
| lookup fabric_site_pod_map leaf OUTPUTNEW pod_label fabric_site
| sort - health_flag_max, encap_errors_total, decap_errors_total
| head 500
```

## Visualization

(1) **Timechart** — **sum(encap_errors_total)** and **sum(decap_errors_total)** by **`fabric_site`**. (2) **Table** — **`leaf`**, **`peer_ip`**, **`vni_key`**, **`nve_peer_state`**, **`vni_oper_state`**, **`bgp_neighbor_state`**, **`triage_bucket`**, **`encap_ratio`**. (3) **Sparkline column** — trailing **`encap_errors_total`** per **`leaf`**. (4) **Map/choropleth** — **`region_code`** when present. (5) **Drilldown** — raw **`evidence_lanes`** events filtered to the clicked tuple.

## Known False Positives

**vtep_bounce_during_issu** — In-service software upgrades and supervisor switchovers on Nexus leaves deliberately bounce NVE peers for tens of seconds while forwarding tables reconverge; Splunk shows peer_down spikes that mirror reload banners even though no customer VLAN ever dropped for minutes. Align positives with stamped CHG identifiers, suppress on device tags until BGP Established timers exceed the vendor soak recommendation, and require syslog corroboration that decap_errors_total stayed flat before treating the event as a false crisis.

**nve_counter_wrap_firmware_cutover** — Certain NX-OS cutovers reset 32-bit VXLAN error counters to zero then immediately overflow small test traffic in labs, producing absurd encap_ratio math for one poll. Compare against ASIC snapshots, widen bin span to fifteen minutes during image weekends, and ignore single-bin outliers when REST poll_phase metadata shows firmware_commit_in_progress.

**path_mtu_renegotiation_blip** — Uplinks moving from 10G to 25G or WAN partners adjusting TCP MSS can transiently inflate decap_errors_total while ICMP too-big messages propagate; cross-check with PATH MTU traces and interface error counters on both ends before blaming EVPN signaling.

**evpn_gr_overlay_stale_marker** — BGP graceful restart leaves EVPN routes in STALE or RIB-frozen states that look ominous in dashboards even while VXLAN dataplane keeps forwarding; differentiate using bgp_neighbor GR flags, stale timers, and whether nve_peer_state remained Up across the entire window.

**staging_l2vni_batch_provision** — Automation waves pre-create hundreds of L2VNIs across leaves ahead of tenant attach; brief vni_down flicker appears on unused segments until access ports map in. Gate alerts with staging_l2vni_batch_provision lookups keyed on vni_key ranges tied to known build tickets.

**split_horizon_learn_guard_ramp** — Turning on additional split-horizon or BGP-EVPN proxy parameters forces cache relearns that mimic dataplane_counter_surge until timers expire; validate against recent config deltas and require encap_errors_total growth on more than two consecutive bins.

**rest_poll_phase_skew_dup_peers** — Mis-tuned REST clients occasionally double-post the same poll into HEC, cloning identical peer rows within the same second—Splunk doubles encap_errors_total without physical faults. Deduplicate on leaf, peer_ip, _raw checksum, and sourcetype before paging; fix the forwarder batching bug instead of dispatching bridge engineers.

## References

- [IETF RFC 7432 — BGP MPLS-Based Ethernet VPN](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7432)
- [IETF RFC 8365 — Network Virtualization Using EVPN](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8365)
- [Cisco Nexus 9000 NX-OS VXLAN BGP EVPN Configuration Guide](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/dcn/nx-os/nexus9000/103x/configuration/evpn/cisco-nexus-9000-nx-os-vxlan-bgp-evpn-configuration-guide-103x/m-overview.html)
- [Arista EVPN documentation hub](https://www.arista.com/en/support/documentation/76-evpn)
- [Splunk REST API Modular Input (Splunkbase 1546)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)
